description: You already built a full React chat frontend for dynamo with SSE streaming, theme detection, and localStorage persistence — so plain React basics are skipped. This roadmap pushes toward production-grade React, state management, Next.js, and UI/UX, aimed at your actual end goal: dashboards for AI apps, real-time data, and agent control panels.

topics.title: Advanced React (hooks, context, performance)
topics.note: You know basic hooks/SSE already — this fills in the patterns that break at scale.

subtopics.title: Custom hooks for shared logic (extract your SSE streaming logic into a reusable useStream hook)
resources: React docs: 'Reusing Logic with Custom Hooks'

subtopics.title: useReducer + useContext for complex local state (before reaching for Redux/Zustand)
resources: Kent C. Dodds - 'How to use React Context effectively'

subtopics.title: Performance: React.memo, useMemo, useCallback — and when they actually help vs add noise
Avoiding re-render storms in streaming UIs (token-by-token updates without janking the whole tree)
resources: Directly apply to your dynamo chat frontend's token stream

subtopics.title: Suspense + concurrent rendering basics (useTransition, useDeferredValue)
Error boundaries & graceful degradation for streaming/network failures

topics.title: State Management (Redux / Zustand)
topics.note: New tools for you — Zustand first (fits your minimal-abstraction style), then Redux for understanding the more verbose alternative.

subtopics.title: Zustand fundamentals: stores, selectors, avoiding unnecessary re-renders
Rebuild your dynamo chat state (messages, streaming status, theme) in Zustand instead of local state/localStorage
Redux Toolkit fundamentals: slices, thunks, RTK Query for data fetching
When to use global state vs server state (React Query/TanStack Query) — critical for agent control panels with live backend data
Persisting and hydrating state (localStorage/IndexedDB middleware) the right way

topics.title: Next.js (SSR + fullstack React)
topics.note: New framework for you, high leverage since it replaces a lot of your Django+React split for frontend-heavy projects.

subtopics.title: App Router fundamentals: file-based routing, layouts, server vs client components
Data fetching patterns: server components, streaming SSR, and how it differs from your Django SSE setup
API routes / route handlers — building a lightweight BFF layer in front of your Django API
Streaming responses in Next.js (React Server Components streaming) — compare to your existing SSE implementation
Auth patterns in Next.js (middleware, session handling) for a real multi-user agent dashboard
Deployment: Vercel vs self-hosted (Docker) — ties into your DevOps learning track

topics.title: UI/UX Basics (responsive, accessibility)
topics.note: Not your strength area yet — enough here to make dashboards usable and credible, not to become a designer.

subtopics.title: Responsive layout systems: CSS Grid + Flexbox mastery beyond basic usage
Design tokens & consistent spacing/typography scales for dashboard UIs
Accessibility fundamentals: semantic HTML, ARIA roles, keyboard navigation, color contrast
Data-dense UI patterns: tables, virtualized lists, real-time updating charts (for monitoring dashboards)
Dark/light theming systems done properly (you've done basic detection — now design tokens + CSS variables approach)
resources: Extend your existing dynamo theme detection into a full design system

topics.title: Goal Projects: Dashboards for AI apps, real-time data, agent control panels
topics.note: Where all the above converges — build these once the prerequisite topics are far enough along.

subtopics.title: Build a monitoring dashboard for dynamo training runs (loss curves, checkpoints, live metrics from Kaggle logs)
Rebuild the dynamo chat frontend in Next.js with Zustand state and streaming server components
Build an agent control panel for your Groq multi-agent coding tool: live tool-call feed, approval/interrupt controls, run history
resources: Self-directed project, tied to your multi-agent security-review tool

subtopics.title: Add real-time updates via WebSockets/SSE to the agent dashboard (task status, streaming tool outputs)
